title: Admin Lock and Unlock User Account
description: The system shall allow an admin to lock a user's account due to suspicious activity and later unlock it when the issue is resolved.
rationale: To manage user account security and access effectively, allowing admins to control user access based on identified issues.
business_value: Improves overall system security by enabling quick response to potential threats and enhancing user trust in the platform's management capabilities.

source.description: As a admin I need to lock a user's account (e.g., due to suspicious activity) and later unlock it when the issue is resolved So that I can manage user account security and access

acceptance_criteria: Given a user account with suspicious activity
When the admin locks the account
Then the account is locked and the user cannot log in
When the admin resolves the issue and unlocks the account
Then the account is unlocked and the user can log in again
preconditions: The system must have an active user account
The system must be able to identify suspicious activity
The admin must have the necessary permissions to lock/unlock accounts
postconditions: The user's account is locked and the user cannot log in when the admin decides to lock it due to suspicious activity
The user's account is unlocked and the user can log in again when the admin resolves the issue and unlocks it
functional_constraints: The system must track the reason for locking the account
The system must provide a mechanism for admins to lock/unlock accounts
The system must notify affected users of their account status change
security_requirements: The system must implement strong access control measures to prevent unauthorized account locking/unlocking
The system must maintain logs of all lock/unlock actions for auditing purposes

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should respond within 5 seconds to lock or unlock requests
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available at least 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must be able to handle up to 1,000 account locking/unlocking requests per minute without degradation in performance
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable

edge_cases: What happens if an admin attempts to lock a non-existent account?
How should the system handle multiple concurrent lock/unlock requests for the same account?
What happens if the system fails to log the reason for locking an account?

interfaces.name: AccountManagementAPI
interfaces.description: Provides endpoints for locking, unlocking, and retrieving account status.

interfaces.name: UserDatabase
interfaces.description: Stores user information, including lock status and reasons for lockout.

test_cases.description: Verify that an admin can successfully lock a user account due to suspicious activity.
test_cases.expected_result: The account is locked, and the user cannot log in.

test_cases.description: Verify that an admin can unlock a previously locked user account when the issue is resolved.
test_cases.expected_result: The account is unlocked, and the user can log in again.

test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to lock a non-existent account results in an error message.
test_cases.expected_result: A clear error message indicating the account does not exist is returned.
